english: people Ld.-Glr.; slób-dpon-gyi (slob dpon gyi) rjés-su (rjes su) brjód-de (brjod de) saying after the teacher Thgy. — rjés-su (rjes su) °dzín-pa ('dzin pa) to receive Pth.: ḳól-por (khol por) rjés-su (rjes su) bzúṅ-nas (bzung nas) lto-gós-kyis (lto gos kyis) bskyáṅ-du (bskyang du) gsol (gsol) pray take me (the orphan) into your service, and provide me with food and clothes; to receive as a disciple or follower = čéd-du (ched du) °dzín-pa ('dzin pa) frq.; to draw after (after death) Mil.; to assist, °dí ('di) rjés-su (rjes su) zuṅ (zung) žig (zhig) do take care of, or provide for this man (as a future co-disciple) Mil.; finally with respect to charms and spells: to commit to memory or keep in memory ni f. — rjés-su (rjes su) °júg-pa ('jug pa) 1. vb. a. to add, affix, 2. vb. n. to follow, bdag (bdag) daṅ (dang) bdág-gi (bdag gi) rjés-su (rjes su) °júg-pai ('jug pa'i) slób-ma-rnams (slob ma rnams) I and the disciples that follow me Mil.; in a similar sense: mi-la (mi la) rjés-su (rjes su) slób-pa (slob pa) to follow another as a disciple Dzl. 250, 3 (262, 7 seems to be a corrupt reading). Also in the following phrases rjés-su (rjes su) may be understood in the sense of: afterwards, subsequently: rjés-su (rjes su) drán-pa (dran pa) to remember, recollect, keep in mind, rjés-su (rjes su) drán-par (dran par) byéd-pa (byed pa) to bring to one's remembrance, to remind Pth.; rjés-su (rjes su) gyód-pa (gyod pa) to repent Cs.; pleon. or without any obvious meaning in: rjés-su (rjes su) mtḥún-pa (mthun pa) Thgy. to agree, to accord, rjés-su (rjes su) rnyéd-pa (rnyed pa) Stg. to find, rjés-su (rjes su) dpág-pa (dpag pa) to weigh, to ponder Cs., rjés-su (rjes su) snyíṅ-brtsé-ba (snying brtse ba) Thgy to pity, rjés-su (rjes su) bstán-pa (bstan pa) Tar. to instruct, and thus in similar expressions, esp. in one of frq. occurrence in legends: rjés-su (rjes su) yi-ráṅ-ba (yi rang ba), resp. rjés-su (rjes su) tḥugs-ráṅ-ba (thugs rang ba) (Sch. erron. tḥugs-pa! (thugs pa!)) to rejoice, to enjoy, for which sometimes also rjés-su (rjes su) pyógs-pa (phyogs pa) is used, e.g. dbyé-ba-rnams-la (dbye ba rnams la) rjés-su (rjes su) yi-ráṅ-ba (yi rang ba) to rejoice at people disagreeing, to enjoy dissensions and jarrings Sty. Comp. rjes-skyés (rjes skyes) (अनुज) born later; younger brother. — rjes-grúb-kyi (rjes grub kyi) miṅ (ming) by-name, surname Cs. — rjes-°júg (rjes 'jug) 1. following, coming after, pyi-rábs (phyi rabs) rjes-°júg (rjes 'jug) tḥams-čád (thams cad) all the following generations Pth. 2. final consonant. — rjes-tḥóg (rjes thog) prob. the same as rjés-la (rjes la) Wdn. — rjes-tḥób (rjes thob) Mil. is said to denote short interruptions of meditation by taking food, but no more than is absolutely necessary for the preservation of life. — rjes-dpág (rjes dpag) 1. consideration, deliberation. 2. Was. (297) a syllogism consisting of three propositions. — rjes-ma (rjes ma) = rjes (rjes) 2 hinder part Cs. — rjes-méd (rjes med) without leaving any traces, trackless, °jig-pa ('jig pa) to destroy thoroughly Glr.

pf. and fut. brjod (brjod), to say, pronounce, utter, e.g. a charm or magic formula; ṅe (nge) miṅ (ming) žód-da (zhod da) rag (rag) W. I hear my name mentioned; saṅs-rgyás-kyi (sangs rgyas kyi) mtsḥán-nas (mtshan nas) to pronounce or invoke the name of Buddha Dzl.; to propound, promulgate, čos (chos) a religious doctrine; to enumerate, set forth, légs-pa (legs pa) or nyés-pa (nyes pa) the good or bad qualities, actions etc., yón-tan (yon tan) the excellence or superiority of a person Dzl. and elsewh.; to treat of a subject in writing: lḥág-pa-rnams (lhag pa rnams) ni (ni) °dir ('dir) brjód-bya (brjod bya) we have now to treat of the rest Zam.; an author even says žes (zhes) bržód-de (brjod de) with regard to his own words (after a bombastic poetical exordium, like the 'dixi', of Roman orators) Glr.; rjód-du (rjod du) méd-pa (med pa) unspeakable, inexpressible, ineffable, rjód-du (rjod du) méd-čiṅ (med cing) dpág-tu (dpag tu) méd-pa (med pa) id. Dzl.; brjod (-kyis) (brjod kyis) mi (mi) láṅ-ba (lang ba) (or lón-ba (lon ba)) id.; also vb.: to be inexpressible or inexhaustible, frq.; re-réï (re re'i) míṅ-nas (ming nas) rjod (rjod) mi (mi) laṅ (lang) one cannot mention or enumerate them all Mil.; don (don) mdzád-pa (mdzad pa) rjod (rjod) mi (mi) láṅ-ṅo (lang ngo) his utility is beyond description Dzl.; rjód-kyis (rjod kyis) mi (mi) láṅ-baï (lang ba'i) pyir (phyir) mi (mi) bkod (bkod) I do not write it down, because it is impossible to relate every thing Pth. (v. brjod (brjod)).
resp. for lče (lce), tongue, ljágs-kyis (ljags kyis) čab (chab) °dór-ba ('dor ba) to spit, to spit out; ljags-čáb (ljags chab) spittle, saliva; ljags-dbúgs (ljags dbugs) breath.
p. n. of a district 1. in Ü, 2. in Kams.

or ljáṅ-gu (ljang gu) Lt., W., green (gen. expressed by ṅón-po (ngon po), notwithstanding the ambiguity), ljaṅ-skyá (ljang skya) greenish white, ljaṅ-nág (ljang nag) greenish black, dark green. — ljáṅ-pa (ljang pa) green corn, in the first stage of its growth (in the second stage it is
